verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II single-pass TM Golgi-resident glycosyltransferase — its canonical home is the Golgi apparatus where it synthesizes lactosamine on glycoconjugates. However, it is well-documented to have a cell-surface form: on sperm and on fertilizing eggs a surface pool of B4GALT1 acts as a zona pellucida receptor, mediating sperm-egg binding. Additionally, in lactating mammary epithelium it forms a complex with alpha-lactalbumin. This tissue-restricted surface display (germline/reproductive lineage and specialized epithelium) qualifies as contextual/tissue_restricted_surface. The dominant compartment is Golgi/endomembrane, but the documented outer-leaflet pool in those specific biological contexts precludes a 'no' verdict.
